title: ⚖️ My Weight
setupDesc: To calculate your personalized recommendations, we need some information.
confidential: 💡 This data is confidential and only used to personalize your recommendations.
howItWorksDesc:
This range is calculated based on your BMI and IOM (National Academies) recommendations used by health professionals.

📊 These are indicative benchmarks, not strict goals!

🤰 Every pregnancy is unique - these figures vary based on many factors.

💛 Talk to your midwife or doctor for personalized monitoring.
startTrackingDesc: Record your first weight to see your progress
bodyChanges: Your body is changing to grow baby! 👶
goalDesc: Ensure you and baby are healthy!
consultDoctor: ⚠️ Consult your doctor for personalized monitoring.
unitCheckMsg:
You entered {{weight}}. Is this in kilograms (kg)?

If it's in pounds (lb), it equals {{converted}} kg.
convertedMsg:
{{original}} lb = {{converted}} kg

You can now save.
highWeightMsg:
{{weight}} kg seems high. Did you maybe enter pounds (lb)?

{{weight}} lb = {{converted}} kg
invalidWeight: Please enter a valid weight between 35 and 200 kg
entryExistsMsg: You already recorded {{weight}} kg today. Do you want to replace this value?
lossMsg:
You lost {{loss}} kg. Significant weight loss during pregnancy may require medical attention.

Talk to your doctor or midwife soon.
variationMsg:
That's a {{variation}} kg difference from your last weigh-in ({{last}} kg). Maybe a typo?

If correct, no worries!
savedMsg: Your weight has been added. Keep it up! 💚
invalidHeight: Please enter a valid height (100-250 cm)
resetConfirmMsg: Do you want to delete this entry?
resetDoneMsg: All your entries have been deleted.

errors.bpRequired: Systolic and diastolic pressure are required
errors.bpDiastolicHigher: Diastolic pressure must be lower than systolic

status.t1Nausea: It's common to plateau or lose a bit of weight in the 1st trimester due to nausea. Don't worry, just stay hydrated! 💛
status.lowSignificant: Your weight is a little below the indicative zone. No worries, every pregnancy is unique! Mention it to your doctor at your next visit. 💛
status.lowSlight: Slightly below the indicative zone — this is often normal, especially in the 1st trimester. Talk to your doctor if you have nausea. 💛
status.highSignificant: Your weight is a little above the indicative zone. Don't panic, these numbers are just guidelines! Mention it to your doctor. 💛
status.highSlight: Slightly above the indicative zone — water retention is common in late pregnancy. Talk to your doctor. 💛
status.normal: Your progress is within the indicative zone, great! Keep taking care of yourself. 💚

trend.stable: Your weight has been stable in recent weeks 📊
trend.rapidGain: Rapid gain detected (+{{rate}}kg/week). Talk to your doctor. ⚠️
trend.rapidLoss: Weight loss detected ({{rate}}kg/week). See your doctor. ⚠️

suggestions.t1Nausea: Frequent nausea? Split your meals into small portions
suggestions.t1Vitamins: Take your prenatal vitamins every day
suggestions.t2Protein: Focus on proteins (meat, fish, eggs) and calcium
suggestions.t2Walk: 30 minutes of walking per day is recommended
suggestions.t3Hydration: Stay hydrated (2L/day) to reduce water retention
suggestions.t3Rest: Rest regularly with your feet elevated
suggestions.statusLow: Increase your portions slightly if your doctor recommends it
suggestions.statusHigh: Focus on vegetables and fiber for satiety
suggestions.rapidGain: See your doctor or midwife soon
suggestions.stable: Keep it up, your balance is great!
suggestions.listenBody: Listen to your body and your hunger/fullness cues

trimester.t1: 1st trimester: Variations are normal with nausea. Stay hydrated! 💛
trimester.t2: 2nd trimester: Baby is growing fast. Balanced nutrition is key! 🌱
trimester.t3: 3rd trimester: Water retention is common. Rest and hydrate! 💧
